# Decisions: staleness-detection

- 2026-07-11T07:21:31.953Z — chose **Derive staleness at fold time from events already in the log and surface it through the existing projections (the shared renderStatus seam already feeds the SessionStart block and the get_state digest; renderFullStatus feeds sofar status)** over New event types (e.g. staleness_marked), a background watcher/daemon that stamps freshness, or hook-side timestamp bookkeeping because The log already carries every needed signal — ulid/ts event order, mechanical events after the last session_ended, and the task-vs-phase divergence doctor D-P11 already detects — and capture burden must stay zero (the record's core discipline): staleness detection that demands new writes would itself go stale, while read-side derivation is compaction-proof and retroactively covers every existing record.
- 2026-07-11T07:53:18.052Z — chose **Staleness plan approved (founder GO, Jul 11) with a binding review constraint: every staleness flag derives from MECHANICAL signals only — next_action age, mechanical events after last write-back, unclosed sessions, task-vs-phase divergence, render-side clip detection** over Any inference of staleness from content semantics (e.g. judging whether the next_action text still makes sense against the code) because Content-semantic inference is the guessing banned at D3/D12 — a confidently wrong staleness verdict is worse than none; mechanical signals are deterministic, replayable, and free from the log
- 2026-07-11T08:11:30.957Z — chose **Extend SPEC §State/§MCP tools/§Hooks/§CLI with the freshness contract (events_since_writeback by kind + last_writeback_ts, session_ended as the only reset, staleActivePhases as the shared D-P11 detector) and the exact surfacing rules per surface (budgeted line in SessionStart/digest, uncapped Staleness section in sofar status, in-budget clipped-summary pointer)** over Leaving staleness behavior implementation-defined outside the SPEC, or documenting only the two surfaces the task named (digest + SessionStart) while sofar status changed too because docs/SPEC.md is authoritative over improvisation (CLAUDE.md) — the staleness signals are now cross-surface contracts consumed by hooks, MCP and CLI alike, and every surface whose output changed (including sofar status under §CLI) must have its contract updated in the same pass or the SPEC silently drifts from the code it governs
